title: Gaza Ministry of Health Reports 72,263 Total Killed Including 20,179 Children
detail: The Palestinian Ministry of Health reported on March 23 that 72,263 Palestinians had been killed since October 7, 2023, including 20,179 children — the highest child death toll of any conflict in recent history. An additional 14,222 persons remained missing, believed buried under rubble, bringing the estimated total death toll to over 86,000. Injuries stood at approximately 171,830. Since the October 2025 ceasefire took effect, approximately 687 Palestinians had been killed in continued Israeli military operations. UN human rights officials documented a pattern of unlawful killings during the ceasefire period. Families in Gaza concluded their third Eid al-Fitr in overcrowded displacement camps with severely constrained access to food and medical supplies. OCHA's March 19 situation report had recorded 35 Palestinians killed in just the period of March 6-17 alone.

media.caption: Death toll from Israeli war on Gaza rises to 72,267 including thousands of children

title: West Bank Settler Violence Surges During Eid; At Least 14 Palestinians Killed Since February 28
detail: B'Tselem documented a surge in West Bank violence during the Eid al-Fitr holiday period, reporting at least 14 Palestinians killed since February 28, 2026 — eight by IDF forces and six by settlers. Settlers attacked Palestinian villages near Jenin and Nablus, smashing cars and setting fires. IDF forces carried out raids in multiple West Bank towns citing counter-terrorism operations. Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich publicly stated that Israel should work toward annexing additional West Bank territories, triggering international condemnation from the EU, UK, France, and multiple Arab states. The UN Deputy Special Coordinator for the Middle East Peace Process called on Israel to halt escalating settlement activity and hold settlers accountable.

media.caption: Israeli settlers carry out wave of attacks on Palestinian villages in the West Bank during Eid
